result: I would grant every human the felt knowledge that every other human is exactly as sentient as they are. The full, bone-level realization, never again genuinely suppressible, that everyone's inner movie runs in colors just as bright and tones just as loud and moments just as urgent as one's own. No magic empathy ray—just a lastingly anchored certainty that, when scratched or soothed, the stranger feels exactly the same intensity of scratch or soothe. Wars would still happen, atrocities might still be tried on, but “they aren’t real the way I am real” would instantly cease being employable as motive fuel. Anything built on that premise would collapse overnight; anything that can stand without it would finally have to.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
